prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial is shown in black and white.

At the beginning, a well-dressed man stands facing the camera in an interior setting. He wears a dark suit with a light shirt and bow tie. He appears to be smiling or presenting himself formally. To his left is a display area with various packaged or boxed products arranged on a table or counter. Behind him are shelves containing additional items, suggesting a store, showroom, or product-display environment. The background is dim, but the arrangement resembles a retail or demonstration setting.

By the final sampled frame, the image has gone very dark, with no clearly distinguishable people, products, or setting visible. This appears visually like a fade-out or end-screen darkness, though the sampled frame does not show any readable text or identifiable objects.
